title: Employee Gift Card Compliance
description: What legal rules must I follow to give electronic gift cards to employees without violating French tax or social security regulations?
narrative: I am an HR manager at a medium‑sized French company and want to launch an annual electronic gift‑card incentive program. I need to know the exact conditions: eligible events, maximum value per card, card type restrictions, social security exemption criteria, and any regional caveats. Relevant documents will explain Urssaf rules, limits based on the social security ceiling, and guidelines for single‑ vs multi‑brand cards.

title: Sustainable Meal Kits
description: Which meal‑kit services deliver locally sourced, organic ingredients using zero‑waste packaging?
narrative: The user is eco‑conscious and wants to reduce their environmental footprint while supporting local producers. They need information on subscription plans that use recyclable or reusable containers, feature seasonal menus, provide transparent ingredient sourcing, offer carbon‑neutral delivery, and have verifiable sustainability certifications. Relevance criteria include detailed descriptions of ingredient origin, packaging type, delivery carbon footprint, cost tiers, and user reviews that specifically address sustainability practices.

title: Urban Rooftop Savings
description: What are the most effective building material and system combinations for maximizing energy savings on green rooftops in temperate urban environments?
narrative: The user is a city‑planning professional researching green roof retrofits and seeks evidence‑based recommendations for material selection and system design. Relevant documents should be peer‑reviewed studies, engineering reports, policy analyses, and detailed case studies that quantify energy performance—preferably for temperate climates. Non‑scientific anecdotes, generic marketing materials, and unrelated building topics should be excluded.

title: Weekend Dining Options
description: When can a family of six dine at this restaurant on a weekend, and what meals are available?
narrative: The user is planning a weekend visit with six people and requires specific opening times for Saturday and Sunday, reservation policies, and a menu that can accommodate a group. Relevant documents should provide weekend hours, group booking information, inclusive pricing, and meal details; documents that limit service to weekdays or provide no weekend info should be deemed irrelevant.

title: Live Video Compression
description: Which codecs, bitrate settings, and resolution strategies deliver the best balance between bandwidth savings and visual quality for real‑time web streaming?
narrative: The user is a live‑streaming content creator seeking technical guidance on selecting and configuring video compression to minimize bandwidth usage without compromising viewer experience. Relevance requires discussion of modern codecs (e.g., H.264, VP9, AV1), adaptive bitrate techniques, latency trade‑offs, and practical deployment on common streaming platforms. Exclude product advertisements and unrelated consumer topics.

title: Social Media Impact
description: How does algorithmic content curation on social media platforms affect adolescent mental health?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based research linking recommendation algorithms, user engagement practices, and measurable psychological outcomes in adolescents aged 13‑18. Relevance criteria include peer‑reviewed journal articles, systematic reviews, or meta‑analyses published since 2015 that explicitly examine algorithmic features (e.g., retweet/like feedback loops, personalized feed sorting) and mental health indicators (anxiety, depression, self‑esteem, sleep disruption). Non‑English, anecdotal, or industry white papers are considered non‑relevant.

title: Rural Digital Divide
description: What factors contribute to the digital divide affecting rural students’ access to online learning resources, and how can schools mitigate these challenges?
narrative: I am a researcher studying educational equity and I need detailed evidence on infrastructural, socioeconomic, and instructional factors driving the digital divide in rural areas. Relevant documents should discuss broadband availability, device penetration, teacher training, and evidence‑based interventions such as community broadband hubs, subsidized devices, curriculum adaptations, or policy initiatives to close the gap.

title: Boys in Schools
description: What evidence exists of educational policies disproportionately disadvantaging male students, and how have these impacts evolved over the last decade?
narrative: The user is seeking rigorous, evidence‑based information that documents gender‑bias in school curricula, disciplinary practices, and resource allocation, with a focus on male students. Relevance is judged by the presence of peer‑reviewed studies, national or regional education reports, longitudinal datasets, and policy analyses that explicitly compare outcomes for boys and girls. Sources that are purely anecdotal, opinion blog posts, or general news coverage without empirical backing are considered irrelevant.

title: Portfolio Credibility
description: What are reliable methods to validate the authenticity and impact of a digital portfolio presented to potential employers?
narrative: The user seeks practical, industry‑validated ways to prove the genuineness and worth of their online portfolio. Relevance requires concrete verification strategies such as third‑party endorsements, code‑hosting audit trails, digital signatures, and employer studies that demonstrate portfolio effectiveness, while generic portfolio advice or internal project lists are considered irrelevant.

title: Sustainable Fashion Online
description: I want to find reputable online stores that offer sustainably produced clothing, providing details on certifications, materials, and shipping practices.
narrative: The user is looking to shop ethically online and needs actionable information about each brand’s sustainability credentials—certifications (e.g., GOTS, Fair Trade, FSC), material sourcing, supply‑chain transparency, and carbon‑footprint metrics. Relevance is judged by the presence of verifiable certification data, transparent supply‑chain details, and realistic shipping/return policies that align with eco‑friendly standards, rather than generic marketing statements or unverified claims.

title: Greek EV Viability
description: How viable should electric vehicles be considered in Greece, given energy sources, incentives, and market trends?
narrative: The user seeks information that covers Greece's electricity mix and pricing, tax and subsidy policies for electric vehicles, infrastructural readiness, consumer attitudes, and current fleet composition. Relevant documents should discuss these factors to assess the practical feasibility and barriers to electric vehicle adoption in the Greek market.

title: Winter Hydroponics
description: How can I modify my hydroponic system to maintain healthy tomato growth during winter months?
narrative: The user is a home grower seeking practical steps to adjust lighting, temperature, nutrient concentration, CO₂ levels, and pest management in a hydroponic setup so that tomato plants remain vigorous when ambient temperatures drop. Relevant documents should provide detailed, experience‑based guidance on winter‑specific practices, equipment adjustments, and risk mitigation for hydroponic tomato cultivation.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What evidence-based urban design strategies effectively reduce heat island intensity and improve public health outcomes in large metropolitan areas?
narrative: The user seeks actionable, peer‑reviewed studies on mitigation practices such as green roofs, reflective pavements, and street trees, along with cost‑benefit analyses and case studies from cities with varied climates. Relevance: documents must report measurable temperature reductions under comparable urban contexts and discuss implementation feasibility.

title: Water‑Resilience in Post‑War Villages
description: What evidence exists that community‑driven water conservation programs improve food security and ecological sustainability in post‑conflict rural settings?
narrative: The user seeks comparative studies, country‑level case reports, and quantitative evaluations that illustrate how post‑conflict communities implement water management initiatives (e.g., rain‑water harvesting, watershed restoration, irrigation scheduling) and the resulting impacts on crop yields, biodiversity, and local livelihoods. Relevance requires documents that: 1) focus on rural settings that have experienced armed conflict; 2) describe water conservation or management interventions; 3) provide measurable outcomes related to food security, ecological health, or economic resilience; 4) include program design, monitoring metrics, and lessons learned. Documents that are purely descriptive of conflict, general water policy without post‑conflict context, or unrelated to rural food systems are considered non‑relevant.

title: Safe Family Superheroes
description: Which superhero movies released between 2015 and 2020 are rated G or PG and have minimal graphic violence?
narrative: The user is a parent seeking a list of superhero films that are suitable for young children.  Relevance is defined by three criteria: (1) the movie was released in the inclusive interval 2015–2020; (2) it carries a G or PG rating (or the equivalent rating in major markets); and (3) it contains only minimal graphic violence, meaning no gratuitous gore, intense fight scenes, or disturbing imagery.  The user also wishes to see a brief plot synopsis for each film and, if possible, information on where the film can be streamed or purchased.

title: Rural Broadband Gap
description: What federal and state policies have been implemented to address broadband connectivity gaps in rural U.S. areas, and what coverage or affordability issues remain?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed accounts of broadband initiatives, subsidy programs, and infrastructure projects aimed at rural communities, including coverage statistics, affordability assessments, and implementation challenges. Relevant documents should describe policy measures, funding mechanisms, or case studies of rural broadband projects; general or urban broadband discussions are not useful.

title: Study Abroad Success
description: How does participating in an Erasmus exchange program influence employability and career trajectories of European students?
narrative: The user seeks empirical, peer‑reviewed research and authoritative policy reports that quantify the impact of Erasmus on skill development, job placement rates, and long‑term career outcomes, while excluding anecdotal evidence or promotional material.

title: Artificial Sand Regulations
description: What are the current environmental and labor regulations governing the production of artificial sand in coastal regions?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on legal frameworks, compliance requirements, and best practices for manufacturers of artificial sand, emphasizing both environmental impact assessments and worker safety standards. Documents that provide specific regulatory texts, case studies of regulatory enforcement, or industry guidelines will be considered relevant; listings of machinery or generic manufacturing advice without regulatory context will not.

title: Safe Women Spaces
description: How can urban planners design public spaces to enhance safety for women and girls?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive design guidelines, best‑practice case studies, and research on lighting, visibility, access, and social design that together foster a sense of security for women and girls in public environments. Relevant documents should contain concrete recommendations, usability criteria, and evidence of effectiveness in real‑world contexts.

title: Green Supplier Comparison
description: How can I evaluate and compare French renewable electricity suppliers to pick the most environmentally friendly and cost‑effective option for my home?
narrative: The user wants a detailed comparison of French green electricity suppliers. Relevance requires information on each supplier’s renewable mix, whether they buy power directly from local producers or rely on Guarantees of Origin, transparency of pricing, contract flexibility (no‑term, cancellation policies), customer support quality, and any ancillary services such as energy‑usage dashboards or community‑energy initiatives. Documents should provide up‑to‑date data and examples of how each supplier’s business model impacts both the environment and the consumer.

title: Procedure X Efficacy Evidence
description: What peer‑reviewed evidence supports the effectiveness of Procedure X for treating Condition Y?
narrative: A medical researcher seeks randomized controlled trials and systematic reviews published from 2015 onward that evaluate Procedure X’s impact on symptom severity, functional outcomes, and quality of life in adults diagnosed with Condition Y.

title: Gallic Warfare Tactics
description: What specific military tactics and organizational structures did Celtic peoples employ during the Roman conquest of Gaul in the 1st century BCE?
narrative: The user is looking for a scholarly synthesis of Celtic military practice, emphasizing battlefield maneuvers, tribal leadership dynamics, and the ways in which these tactics countered or differed from Roman methods. Relevance criteria include references to primary sources or archaeological evidence from the period and a clear focus on the 1st‑century BCE campaigns, excluding modern reinterpretations or generic summaries of Celtic culture.

title: Cross-Border Rental Regulations
description: What insurance, fee, and legal requirements apply when renting a car in one European country and driving across the border into another?
narrative: The user intends to rent a car at an airport in Germany (or a nearby city) and travel into France or another EU member state. The relevance criteria include information about cross‑border liability coverage, mandatory insurance add‑ons, toll and tax obligations, any changes to rental agreements for international travel, and how varying national regulations affect the renter. Generic domestic rental details, unrelated promotional offers, or non‑cross‑border insurance policies should be excluded.

title: Urban Green Roofs
description: What are the best practices and environmental benefits of installing green roofs in dense city environments?
narrative: The user is researching sustainable building practices, specifically green roofs, to understand design guidelines, energy savings, and ecological impacts in metropolitan settings. Relevance is judged by content that discusses technical criteria, policy incentives, cost-benefit analyses, and case studies of successful implementations.

title: Potato Climate Adaptation
description: What strategies can farmers use to cultivate potatoes resilient to rising temperatures and water scarcity?
narrative: The user is a potato farmer seeking evidence‑based practices to adapt cultivation to climate change, including heat‑tolerant varieties, irrigation techniques, soil amendments, and agroecological approaches; relevant documents should provide scientific reviews, case studies, and practical guidelines applicable to European climate conditions.

title: Beaujolais Vineyards
description: Which local vineyards in the Beaujolais region offer well-regarded wine tastings and tours?
narrative: The user wants a list of specific vineyards recognized for quality, including their locations, contact info, tasting prices, and any special events. Relevance requires documents that mention particular vineyard names and provide practical tour or tasting details, rather than general region overviews.

title: Low‑Impact River Travel
description: What are sustainable options for traveling by river that minimize environmental impact?
narrative: The user is researching ecologically responsible water travel. They want comprehensive information on boat design, propulsion technology, fuel use, and lifecycle environmental assessments. Relevance includes details about low‑carbon or renewable power sources, certification standards, safety measures, and community partnership programs on river routes. Items that merely mention leisure boating or do not address sustainability metrics should be considered non‑relevant.

title: Transporter Tire Reset
description: I need step‑by‑step instructions to reset the tire pressure warning light on my Volkswagen Transporter T4 after inflating all tires.
narrative: The user is a Volkswagen Transporter T4 owner who has recently inflated all tires and wants to turn off the tire pressure sensor glow on the dashboard. Relevance criteria: provide specific reset steps for the T4, mention dashboard menus or button holds, include any year‑specific variations, and offer troubleshooting if the light persists. Exclude generic tire‑pressure light instructions for other vehicles, outdated methods, or unrelated maintenance tips.

title: High‑Altitude Agri‑Sustainability
description: What sustainable farming methods are most effective for smallholder farmers living above 2500 m altitude?
narrative: The user seeks practical, research‑based recommendations for crop selection, soil conservation, water‑efficient irrigation, and climate‑resilience techniques suitable for high‑altitude settings. Relevance is limited to documents that provide evidence from case studies, regional guidelines, or agronomic trials in similar environments; documents that merely mention altitude or offer generic advice without actionable guidance should be excluded.

title: Microcredit and Girls' Education
description: How do microcredit initiatives influence girls' school enrollment, attendance, and completion rates in Sub‑Saharan African communities?
narrative: The information seeker is a development researcher exploring the causal relationship between microcredit access and female education outcomes. Relevance is confined to peer‑reviewed evaluations, secondary data analyses, observational studies, and program reports that directly assess enrollment, attendance, completion, and related socioeconomic indicators, while excluding general macroeconomic overviews or unrelated gender studies.

title: Renewable-Grid Stability Integration
description: How can we design control strategies that maintain voltage and frequency stability while integrating high shares of intermittent renewable generation into modern power grids?
narrative: The user seeks detailed technical approaches, including control theory, market mechanisms, and case studies, to preserve grid reliability under variable wind and solar outputs. Relevance requires sources that discuss dynamic stability, advanced inverter controls, or policy frameworks applied in actual grids.

title: Public vs Ride‑Share Energy
description: How does the energy consumption per passenger kilometer compare between public transit and ride‑sharing vehicles in urban areas?
narrative: The user wants detailed comparative data on energy use per passenger kilometer for public transport (buses, subways) versus ride‑sharing cars in major cities, including methodology, data sources, and limitations. Relevance is judged by the inclusion of quantitative metrics, discussion of vehicle occupancy, route efficiency, and potential confounding factors.

title: Wheelchair‑Accessible Bordeaux Buses
description: Identify all Bordeaux bus routes that are wheelchair‑accessible and provide their schedules.
narrative: The user is a senior with limited mobility who needs to plan trips for shopping and medical appointments. They require bus routes that are fully wheelchair‑accessible, featuring lifts or ramps, and they want up‑to‑date schedules, especially for midday non‑stop service, to avoid transfers and long waits.

title: Medieval Manuscript Research
description: What primary resources are available for researching medieval manuscripts in European libraries?
narrative: The user is an academic scholar seeking authoritative sources—digital repositories, peer‑reviewed journal articles, and official library catalogs—pertaining to medieval manuscript collections across European libraries. Relevant documents must provide scholarly insight or direct access to primary holdings; general blogs, non‑academic listings, or non‑related sites are excluded.

title: Digital Identity Security
description: What are the best practices for protecting personal digital identities across multiple online platforms, and how can individuals assess the risk of identity theft in different contexts?
narrative: The user seeks practical, detailed guidance on securing personal digital identity, including multi‑factor authentication, password strategies, biometric privacy, monitoring services, and legal protection. Relevance is judged by the presence of actionable steps for individuals, assessments of risk levels for various scenarios (e.g., banking, social media, government services), and comparisons of tools and practices rather than generic or purely theoretical discussion.

title: Green Flexible Offices
description: I am looking for environmentally certified office spaces in major European cities that offer flexible lease terms and are located close to public transportation.
narrative: The user is a sustainability‑conscious entrepreneur who needs medium‑sized office space (5‑15 people) meeting LEED, BREEAM, or similar green building certification, with short‑term or month‑to‑month lease options, and situated within a short walk of a metro or rail station.

title: Venezuelan Currency Evolution
description: What have been the major milestones in the history of Venezuelan currency, from the Bolivar to the current digital banking era?
narrative: The user seeks a concise historical outline of Venezuelan monetary evolution, including the establishment of the Central Bank, changes in currency names and denominations, transition between silver, gold, and nickel compositions, the shift to the bolívar fuerte and subsequent demonetizations, key dates and policy shifts, and the impact of hyperinflation and transition toward digital banking. This information will be used for a research paper that requires an accurate timeline and contextual understanding of the socioeconomic implications.

title: Savoy Wine Pairing
description: Which Savoy wines best accompany vegetarian, flexitarian, and vegan dishes during a weekend gathering?
narrative: The user plans a weekend dinner for a mixed dietary group (vegetarian, flexitarian, vegan). They need wine recommendations that match specific dishes such as roasted vegetables, fish, and plant‑based meals, considering flavor profile, pairing guidelines, and seasonal suitability.

title: AI News Impact
description: What is the impact of AI-generated news on public trust and the spread of misinformation?
narrative: The user seeks to understand how automated news content influences public attitudes toward news credibility and the prevalence of misinformation. Relevant evidence must come from scholarly research, empirical surveys, or reputable policy analyses published from 2018 onward and should exclude entertainment or gaming sources.

title: Quantum Entanglement Experiments
description: What recent experimental evidence supports the existence of quantum entanglement?
narrative: The user is a graduate student researching quantum information science who wants to locate peer‑reviewed articles and conference proceedings that describe state‑of‑the‑art experiments demonstrating entanglement, including loophole‑free Bell tests, multi‑photon entanglement, and practical implementations in quantum communication and computing. Relevance should be judged by publication date (2015‑present), the presence of experimental data, and the discussion of implications for quantum technologies.

title: Data Consumption Monitoring
description: How can I accurately track and manage my mobile data usage on Free Mobile when typical SMS-based tracking is unreliable and I occasionally lack internet access?
narrative: The user seeks reliable methods to monitor data consumption, understand limits, receive alerts, and access usage information independently of a constant internet connection. Documents that describe SMS services, app functionality, modem settings, and alternative monitoring tactics are considered relevant.

title: Cardan Lamp History
description: Seeking comprehensive details on the design, history, and manufacturing of Cardan lamps, including materials, functionality, and distinctions from other oil lamps.
narrative: The user wants to gather historical and technical information about Cardan lamps, their structural features (pivot, beak, tanks), origins, material types (bronze, glass), manufacturing techniques, usage patterns (reading, decorative), and visual documentation. Relevance requires content that explains the Cardan lamp’s unique characteristics, provenance, and comparison to other lamps.

title: Cross-border Rental Insurance
description: What insurance is required when renting a car in Calais and driving into Belgium, and how can I avoid extra fees?
narrative: The user is planning to rent a vehicle in Calais, France, and drive into neighboring Belgium. They want detailed information on mandatory insurance coverage across EU borders, any additional fees or restrictions for cross-border travel, and practical advice on selecting the most cost‑effective and compliant insurance plan. Relevance is judged by the answer’s ability to clarify legal requirements, explain how to avoid hidden charges, and suggest specific insurance options or providers that cover both countries.

title: Late Blight Management
description: What are effective strategies to prevent and treat late blight in home-grown potatoes to reduce crop loss?
narrative: The user is a home gardener or small farmer looking for practical, evidence-based methods—including resistant varieties, cultural practices, and organic treatments—to control Phytophthora infestans outbreaks. Relevance requires up-to-date horticultural guidance, research findings, and actionable tips for low-input agriculture.

title: Bias Audit Standards
description: How can organizations conduct comprehensive institutional audits to identify and mitigate algorithmic bias in their machine learning systems?
narrative: The user is a data scientist seeking structured guidelines and best practices for institutional bias audits, including stakeholder roles, methodological choices, validation metrics, and actionable remediation steps.
